mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months with declining PAYEMS, or the correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer and construction demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS + HOUST both rising) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and reduce forward earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances where INDPRO declines >0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off sentiment and equity selloffs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >0.3 percentage points in 1 month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
regime_hint: recession or sharp labor market shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (UNRATE rising + PAYEMS declining) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals persistent inflation and tight monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals forward-looking confidence in residential construction demand and household formation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical equity sectors respond to improved housing outlook.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of months with rising HOUST, or the correlation between HOUST monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply-side and labor market expansion that supports earnings growth and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations strengthen.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises >0.3% month-over-month AND PAYEMS increases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or mid-cycle expansion with synchronized growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (INDPRO rising + PAYEMS rising) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
